summary: Comprehensive testing of JWT-based email/password authentication for mumble language tutoring app. All 17 backend API tests passed (100%) and all 20 frontend UI tests passed (100%). Authentication system is fully functional.
passed_tests: POST /api/auth/signup with name, email, password returns token + user object (200)
POST /api/auth/signup with duplicate email returns 409 Conflict
POST /api/auth/signup with short password (<6 chars) returns 400 error
POST /api/auth/signup with invalid email format returns 422 validation error
POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials returns token + user (demo@mumble.app)
POST /api/auth/login with wrong password returns 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/auth/login with non-existent email returns 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/auth/login immediately after signup works correctly
GET /api/auth/me with valid Bearer token returns user info
GET /api/auth/me without token returns 403 Forbidden
GET /api/auth/me with invalid token returns 401 Unauthorized
GET /api/conversations without token returns 403 Forbidden
GET /api/conversations with valid token returns user's conversations list
GET /api/vocabulary without token returns 403 Forbidden
GET /api/progress without token returns 403 Forbidden
POST /api/conversations without token returns 403 Forbidden
User data isolation - each user sees only their own conversations
Auth page (/auth) loads with login form showing email/password fields
Auth page has tab switcher between 'Log In' and 'Sign Up'
Sign Up tab shows additional Name field with smooth animation
Google button placeholder is visible and correctly disabled with 'coming soon' text
Signup flow: fill form -> submit -> redirect to /chat with user email in sidebar
Login flow: fill form (demo@mumble.app) -> submit -> redirect to /chat
Error handling: wrong password displays 'Invalid email or password' error
Protected route: accessing /chat without auth redirects to /auth
Protected route: accessing /dashboard without auth redirects to /auth
Protected route: accessing /vocabulary without auth redirects to /auth
Sidebar shows user email (data-testid='sidebar-user-email')
Logout button (data-testid='sidebar-logout-btn') clears auth and redirects
Landing page (/) is accessible without authentication
Landing page 'Start Free' button redirects to /auth for unauthenticated users
Toggle mode link switches between login/signup modes correctly
critical_code_review_comments: JWT implementation uses PyJWT + passlib/bcrypt - secure password hashing
Token expiry set to 72 hours (JWT_EXPIRY_HOURS = 72) - reasonable for UX
Email is normalized to lowercase on signup/login - prevents duplicate accounts
Password minimum length validation (6 chars) implemented server-side
HTTPBearer security scheme properly requires Authorization header
All data routes (conversations, vocabulary, progress) properly scoped by user_id
Frontend stores token in localStorage with key 'mumble_token'
React context (useAuth) properly manages auth state and token refresh on mount
ProtectedRoute component correctly redirects to /auth when not authenticated
Error messages displayed clearly via data-testid='auth-error'
All interactive elements have data-testid attributes for testing
seed_data_creation: None required - demo@mumble.app account already exists
context_for_next_testing_agent: JWT auth fully implemented and tested. Backend uses PyJWT with HS256, passwords hashed with bcrypt. Frontend uses React context (useAuth) with localStorage. All protected routes (/chat, /dashboard, /vocabulary) require auth. Auth page at /auth with data-testids: auth-tab-login, auth-tab-signup, auth-name-input, auth-email-input, auth-password-input, auth-submit-btn, auth-error, auth-google-btn (disabled placeholder), auth-toggle-mode, auth-logo. Sidebar shows user email (sidebar-user-email) and logout button (sidebar-logout-btn). Token stored in localStorage as 'mumble_token'. Demo account: demo@mumble.app / demo123. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_auth.py with 17 test cases covering signup, login, token verification, protected routes, and user data isolation.

signup.returns: token + user object with id, name, email

login.error_handling: 401 for wrong password or non-existent email
